definition: Effective immigration restriction systematically undershoots headline restriction across all documented cases, but the self-correction thesis has only been tested during extremely tight labor markets (2021-2024). Whether liberalization survives a recession is untested.
evidence: UK post-Brexit net migration doubled (745K vs 270K pre-Brexit)
Japan 10x'd foreign workers while maintaining closed-society narrative
Canada carved sector exceptions within 12 months
4/4 documented cases show headline restriction + effective liberalization
Persistence CI spans [12%, 88%] from N=6-7 — too wide for high confidence
Cycle compression from 18 years (Gastarbeiter) to ~3 years may be overfitted to two data points
open_questions: Does self-correction survive a recession when political economy flips against immigration?
Is the 3-year cycle compression robust or overfitted?
Does selective low-skilled vs high-skilled restriction change the dynamics?
